vocab.meaning: vun đắp/khơi dậy niềm yêu thích
vocab.example: My primary school teacher really managed to instill a love for oil painting in me through her engaging workshops.

vocab.meaning: sự phối hợp giữa tay và mắt
vocab.example: I was told that practicing sketching at a young age is a fantastic way to improve hand-eye coordination.

vocab.meaning: nguyên lý/học thuyết về màu sắc
vocab.example: I remember being fascinated by color theory and how certain shades could completely alter the mood of a drawing.

vocab.meaning: những nỗ lực/hoạt động nghệ thuật
vocab.example: My parents were incredibly supportive of my artistic endeavors, providing me with all the canvases and brushes I needed.

vocab.meaning: vẽ phác thảo bằng than chì
vocab.example: During my early teens, I briefly experimented with charcoal sketching to create more dramatic and textured portraits.

vocab.meaning: nguồn cảm hứng/tia sáng sáng tạo
vocab.example: I often found that listening to music provided the creative spark I needed to start a new watercolor piece.
I wasn't particularly good at realism, so I gravitated towards abstract expression to convey my feelings.
We spent weeks in art class focusing on still-life drawing, usually depicting bowls of fruit or vintage vases.

vocab.meaning: khả năng cảm thụ thẩm mỹ
vocab.example: Learning to paint helped refine my aesthetic sensibilities, making me more aware of the beauty in mundane objects.

vocab.meaning: những nỗ lực mang tính nghiệp dư
vocab.example: Although my early paintings were merely amateurish attempts, they gave me a great deal of personal satisfaction.
